name: Potes to Fuente Dé Cycle Route
name_original: Potes, Espinama, Fuente Dé – vuelta siguiendo el Río Deva
description: This difficult 28.9-mile road cycling route from Potes to Fuente Dé and back offers stunning Picos de Europa views and a challenging climb.
region_intro: Road cycling around Potes is characterized by its dramatic mountain landscapes and challenging terrain, nestled within the Liébana region and providing access to the Picos de Europa National Park. The area features well-maintained roads with minimal traffic, winding through deep valleys, alongside rivers like the Deva and Quiviesa, and over significant mountain passes. This varied Potes road cycling terrain offers a range of ascents and descents, from moderate climbs to demanding, sustained gradients. Best road cycling routes around Potes The most popular road cycling route is Potes to Fuente Dé Cycle Route, a 28.9 miles (46.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs and offers stunning views of the Picos de Europa. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Glorio to Collada de Llesba CIMA Climb, a difficult 35.2 miles (56.6 km) path. This classic and long ascent gradually builds to steady gradients, offering spectacular views from the highest climb in the Picos de Europa area. Local road cyclists also love the Piedrasluengas Viewpoint – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes, a 70.9 miles (114.1 km) trail leading through the rugged Picos de Europa terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Potes is defined by dramatic mountain passes, deep valleys, and rugged Picos de Europa terrain. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Potes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Potes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Potes?
A: There are over 14 road cycling routes around Potes, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1,400 times, with an average rating of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Potes?
A: Road cycling around Potes is characterized by dramatic mountain landscapes and challenging terrain, with well-maintained roads and minimal traffic. You'll encounter deep valleys, winding roads alongside rivers like the Deva and Quiviesa, and significant mountain passes. The area is renowned for its demanding climbs, including those featured in professional races, but also offers routes with varied ascents and descents.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the Potes area?
A: Absolutely. The region is a true biker's paradise with several iconic climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005126390/san-glorio-to-collada-de-llesba-cima-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio to Collada de Llesba CIMA Climb&lt;/a&gt; is a classic, long ascent that gradually builds to steady gradients, offering spectacular views from the highest climb in the Picos de Europa area. Another notable challenge is the route to Fuente Dé, which features significant climbs and stunning vistas of the Picos de Europa.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often wind through spectacular natural features. You can expect dramatic views of the Picos de Europa National Park, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004911979/potes-to-fuente-de-cycle-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Potes to Fuente Dé Cycle Route&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its deep gorges, such as those around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4599844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo del Hasta Luego&lt;/a&gt;, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5446437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deva River&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the chance to visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/861319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Potes?
A: Yes, Potes serves as an excellent starting point for circular routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 114 km trail that takes you through the rugged Picos de Europa terrain. Another moderate loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camaleño – Historic center of Potes loop from Potes&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 38 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Potes?
A: The ideal seasons for road cycling in Potes are spring and early autumn. During these times, the weather is generally pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. While summer is also viable, it tends to be more popular with tourists, and temperatures can be higher, especially on climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes with lower elevation gain around Potes?
A: While many routes around Potes feature significant climbs due to the mountainous terrain, some sections within the valleys, such as the Liébana and Camaleño Valleys, offer more moderate gradients. For a less strenuous experience, consider exploring shorter loops that stay closer to the valley floors, though truly flat routes are rare in this dramatic landscape.

Q: What can I explore in Potes itself after a ride?
A: Potes is recognized as one of Spain's most beautiful villages. After your ride, you can explore its lively atmosphere, cobblestone streets, and historic buildings like the Torre del Infantado. The town offers plenty of accommodation, local cuisine, and shops. Just outside Potes, you can also visit the Monastery of Santo Toribio de Liébana, an important religious site.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer access to significant landmarks. For instance, the route towards Fuente Dé leads to the famous cable car, which provides quick access to high-altitude landscapes and hiking trails. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3601813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viorna Cross&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6858722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular highlights in the area.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Potes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the well-maintained roads with minimal traffic. The dramatic views of the Picos de Europa and the sense of accomplishment after conquering a major pass are frequently highlighted.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Potes?
A: Potes, being a popular tourist destination and a base for outdoor activities, offers various parking options. You'll find public parking areas within or on the outskirts of the village, suitable for leaving your vehicle while you embark on your cycling adventures. It's advisable to check local signage for any specific restrictions or fees.

Q: Can I use public transport with my bicycle in the Potes region?
A: While public transport options exist in the region, their capacity for carrying bicycles can be limited, especially on smaller buses or during peak seasons. It's recommended to check with local bus operators in advance regarding their bicycle policies and availability, particularly if you plan to use them for longer distances or specific routes.
tips: Nestled at the foot of the Picos de Europa, it is surrounded by spectacular mountains. The people of Espinama are dedicated to livestock and, above all, to the hotel business (taking advantage of the proximity to Fuente Dé and the presence of tourism). It is essential to visit the old church of the town, which is from the 17th century.

A lush and diverse environment with abundant species of birds, ibex and other animals. In addition, the area is perfect for taking photos. And of course, one of the main attractions of Fuente Dé is its cable car, which ascends visitors to an altitude of around 1,823 meters. The cable car ride is spectacular and offers panoramic views.

Potes is one of the most beautiful towns in Cantabria and Spain. It has a privileged position to visit the Picos de Europa. It is near the point of arrival of the Camino Lebaniego and frankly, it is worth staying there at night to rest and take advantage of its restaurants and its small streets.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a difficult road ride and requires a very good level of fitness. It features significant elevation gains, including a Category 1 ascent to Fuente Dé, making it unsuitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily well-paved roads, but it involves substantial climbing, particularly the 22-kilometer ascent to Fuente Dé with an average gradient of 3.5-3.6% and steeper sections reaching 8-9%. The descent back towards Potes along the River Deva is relatively easy with no difficult hairpin bends.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling this route?
faqs.answer: Potes is a common starting point and offers various parking options. You can typically find parking within or near the historic center of Potes, which serves as an excellent base for exploring the Liébana region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 46.5 kilometers long with over 900 meters of elevation gain. For an experienced racebike cyclist, it typically takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete, but this can vary based on fitness and stops.

faqs.question: Are there bike rental options available in Potes for this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Potes is a popular hub for outdoor activities in the Picos de Europa, and you can find several shops offering bike rentals, including road bikes suitable for this route. It's advisable to book in advance, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Potes, Espinama, Fuente De cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through charming villages like Espinama Village and the historic town of Potes. A major highlight is Fuente Dé Cable Car, offering spectacular views of the Picos de Europa. The entire route follows the picturesque River Deva, providing continuous stunning mountain landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the villages along the route, particularly in Potes, Espinama, and Fuente Dé. These are great spots to refuel and enjoy local Cantabrian cuisine.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Potes, Espinama, Fuente De route?
faqs.answer: Spring and early autumn are the ideal seasons for cycling this route, offering pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. While summer is also viable, it tends to be more popular with tourists, and temperatures can be higher.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Picos de Europa National Park. However, always respect park regulations, especially concerning wildlife and waste.

faqs.question: Is the Potes, Espinama, Fuente De cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route follows public roads, which are generally accessible, it's not typically considered a 'dog-friendly trail' in the same way a hiking path might be. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely travel in a bike trailer or is accustomed to long rides on a leash if you stop in villages. Always check local regulations for specific areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other notable paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Transcantábrica, Camino Vadiniense, and the Ruta de la Reconquista (Etapa 4) for significant portions of the ride.
